title: Watermelon
description:
Properties and benefits:  
watermelon Watermelon is the fruit of the watermelon, a herbaceous plant with creeping, hairy stems belonging to the Cucurbitaceae family. This very large, spherical fruit is made up of a hard green rind, which surrounds a sweet and juicy pulp that is more or less reddish in color and can vary depending on the type of fruit. title: Strawberry
description:
Properties and benefits:  
strawberry Strawberries and strawberries (its largest variety) are several species of creeping plants of the Fragaria genus, belonging to the Rosaceae family. Strawberries are characterized by being intensely red and conical in shape, with a crown of small green leaves on top. Rough to the touch, this fruit is a floral receptacle that contains a multitude of seeds called achenes.

title: Redcurrant
description:
Properties and benefits:  
redcurrant The currant is the fruit that comes from a wild bush called currant bush, belonging to the saxifragaceae family. They are small round red fruits that grow in clusters and look similar to grapes, but much smaller in size. Currently we can find 3 different kinds of currants on the market, differentiated by their colour: red currants (the most common), black currants (more acidic than red) and white or pink currants (the less cultivated variety).

title: Raspberry
description:
Properties and benefits:  
raspberry The raspberry is the fruit of a bush belonging to the Rosacea family and which grows in the wild in forests and fields. The raspberry is a polydrupe: in other words, it is a fruit made of up lots of small fruits or small velvety drupes that are inserted into the same receptacle. title: Quince
description:
Properties and benefits:  
quince Quince is the fruit of the quince tree, belonging to the Rosaceae family. While the fruit is immature, its skin is green and has a dense light gray hair, which is lost once it has matured. When this fruit is ready to be harvested, it has a yellow skin and a very aromatic hard pulp. title: Pomegranate
description:
Properties and benefits:  
pomegranate The pomegranate is the fruit of the pomegranate shrub, a deciduous fruit shrub from the Punicoideae family. It is a globular berry with a hard peel and its inside is subdivided into several lobes. Its pulp is red, sweet, fleshy and juicy. It contains numerous seeds inside which are coated in sarcotesta and separated by membrane walls. Some benefits you didn't know about taking

title: Persimmon
description:
Properties and benefits:  
persimmon Persimmon is the berry from a tropical tree from the Ebenaceae family of the same name. This fruit has a smooth, shiny and orange skin, although with some varieties the colour ranges from red to yellow. The flavour of the persimmon varies depending on its ripeness: while the pulp of the unripened fruit has a pungent taste, when the fruit is ripe it takes on a very sweet and smooth flavour.

title: Pear
description:
Properties and benefits:  
pear The pear is the fruit of the pear tree, a tree of the Rosaceae family. This oval fruit is fleshy, with a thin green skin and a sweet, juicy pulp with a mealy texture. Today there are many natural and hybrid varieties of pear, some of which are sweeter than others. Depending on their color, they can be white, yellow, green, red or brown. peach The peach is the fruit of the peach tree, a tree belonging to the Rosaceae family. This fruit is a round drupe with a cleft in the center. Its skin is smooth, orange with reddish tones and velvety to the touch. Its pulp is fleshy and sweet and its interior contains a hard bone. Some benefits you didn't know about taking

orange The orange is the fruit of the sweet orange tree, a citrus tree of the Rutaceae family. This fruit has a round appearance with a firm and shiny orange skin. Its pulp is made up of numerous vesicles filled with juice. The flavor of this fruit varies from acid to sweet. melon The melon is the fruit of the melonera, a herbaceous plant with a creeping stem of the Cucurbitaceae family. Its bark is smooth, shiny, red-orange in color and very easy to peel. Its pulp is made up of numerous vesicles filled with very sweet juice. title: Loquat
description:
Properties and benefits:  
loquat Loquat is the fruit obtained from the tree of the same name, belonging to the Rosaceae family. This fruit is oval shaped and has a thin, hard skin which is orange in colour. Its pulp is fleshy, sweet and somewhat acidic, although it tastes different depending on the variety. Inside, it contains several large brown seeds.

title: Lemon
description:
Properties and benefits:  
lemon The lemon is the berry fruit of the lemon tree, an evergreen tree of the Rutaceae family. The lemon is oblong in shape and covered by a yellow, thick and aromatic skin. Inside we find a very juicy pulp in the form of segments and with a very acid taste. According to their size, lemons can be classified as small, medium and large and, according to their color, green and yellow. Although, in Spain, the most consumed is the large yellow lemon.

title: Kiwi
description:
Properties and benefits:  
kiwi The kiwi is the fruit that is obtained from the actinidia plant, of the family of the actinidiáceas. It is an ovoid-shaped berry fruit covered with a thin brown skin, slightly hairy, which includes a very juicy and acid green pulp (although in some varieties it can be yellow). Today we find different varieties on the market: the kaquiara II, the Actinidia and the hayward. In Spain, the most commercialized variety is the Hayward.

grapefruit The grapefruit is the fruit of the tree that receives the same name and that belongs to the Rutaceae family. This tree forms hermaphroditic flowers that later become small endings that bear the fruit of the grapefruit. This fruit has a spherical shape and a thick yellow rind with pinkish tints. Its pulp is similar to that of an orange and contains numerous vesicles filled with juice, very juicy and with an acid or sweet taste, depending on the variety.

title: Fig
description: The fig is considered a false fruit, since it is a receptacle that contains hundreds of small fruits, which we call pips or seeds. Figs grow on large trees called fig trees, belonging to the Moraceae family. It is a soft fruit with a very thin skin, whose color varies from green to purple. Its pulp is very sweet, it can be reddish or white and it is full of seeds inside, although these are very small and edible. title: Date
description:
Properties and benefits:  
date The date comes from the date palm tree, belonging to the Araceae family. It is a palm tree with a single trunk and covered in leaves (the palms), which sprout cream coloured branched leaflets, on which oval fruits grow (the dates). They are dark orange in colour, with a smooth shell and a fleshy and sweet pulp, similar to honey. Inside they contain a long stone with a lengthways slit.

This fruit is heart-shaped and is covered in a fine green shell with indents in the shape of scales. title: Courgette
description:
Properties and benefits:  
courgette Courgette is the vegetable from a herbaceous plant from the Cucurbitaceae family of the same name. This plant has a coarse appearance and its stems sprout large green serrated leaves, as well as yellow flowers that produce this vegetable. From the courgette plant, not only the vegetable itself is consumed, but its flowers can also be eaten fried and they are an exquisite snack.

title: Chicory
description:
Properties and benefits:  
chicory Chicory is a vegetable belonging to the Asteraceae or Compositae family, which include more than 20,000 species, of which very few are cultivated. Asteraceae are characterised for the fact that their flowers are made up of the fusion of thousands of tiny flowers. This family includes vegetables from different species which are grouped as follows: flowering Asteraceae, stem Asteraceae and leaf Asteraceae.

cherry Cherries are the fruit obtained from the cherry tree, belonging to the Rosacea family. title: Chard

chard Chard is a herbaceous plant belonging to the Chenopodioideae family. It has large green leaves and white petioles called stalks. Both the leaves and stalks are edible parts of this vegetable. Nowadays we can find different varieties of chard on the market, which are classified according to their colour and the size of their leaves.

title: Celery

celery Celery is a vegetable belonging to the Umbelliferae family. This plant has thick green grooved stems that form a stalk with a cluster of ribbed leaves at the end. The name, celery, refers to the species which is eaten for its leaves; therefore, it does not include celeriac or celery root, although they both belong to the same species.

Broccoli is similar to cauliflower, both in shape and flavour, although the latter is white. title: Bell pepper
description:
Properties and benefits:  
bell pepper Peppers are the hollow fruit of the herbaceous plant of the same name, belonging to the Solanaceae family. In modern horticulture, there are 3 large groups of peppers: sweet, chilli and different varieties to make paprika. Bell peppers are characterised for being a thick, fleshy and large variety. The skin of this vegetable is red, fine and shiny, while its flesh is soft and sweet. On the top, they contain a green and rigid stem.

title: Beetroot
description:
Properties and benefits:  
beetroot Beetroot is the deep, large and fleshy root that grows on the plant of the same name and which forms part of the Chenopodioideae family. It is a round and spherical root, the skin on top is fine and soft and can be different colours, from pinkish violet and reddish orange to brown. Its pulp tastes sweet and is usually a dark crimson red colour with purple hues and, sometimes, it can have concentric circles inside.

banana <!--td {border: 1px solid #ccc;}br {mso-data-placement:same-cell;}--> The banana is the fruit of the banana tree or banana, belonging to the Musaceae family. title: Avocado
description:

avocado Avocado is the fruit obtained from the avocado tree, belonging to the Lauraceae family. This fruit has a thick and hard green skin, although the shade can change depending on the variety. The pulp of the avocado is oily and a cream or yellowish-green colour, with a very mild flavour. Inside, it contains one large, round seed which is light brown in colour and can make up half of the size of the fruit.

title: Aubergine
description:

aubergine Aubergine is the vegetable obtained from the aubergine plant, from the Solanaceae family. This plant has a hairy stem with branches leading to hairy leaves that are coarse to touch. Aubergines are variable in shape and can range from being spherical to egg-shaped and oblong. Aubergines are purple in colour, although their intensity will depend on the variety we buy. The flesh is firm and white, and they contain numerous edible seeds of the same colour.

description:

artichoke Artichokes come from the artichoke plant, a perennial plant from the Asteraceae family. Both the plant itself and its edible green cocoon-shaped produce are called artichoke. Artichokes are perennial plants that grow back every year if they haven’t suffered any type of damage from extreme cold or heat.

title: Apple
description:
Properties and benefits:  
apple The apple is the fruit of the apple tree, belonging to the Rosaceae family. Its skin is fine and its color can vary between yellow, green and red, while its interior is always creamy, with a crunchy and floury texture. The flavor of the apple varies depending on its variety and degree of maturity: for example, red and green apples have a slightly more acidic flavor, while yellow apples are much sweeter.

description: La Bodega de Consum is your wine corner where you can discover the world of pairing, wine tasting and designation of origin. Knowing how to choose a good wine may seem difficult, but with some tips it's easy. When making wine, the grape variety is one of the determining factors.
